questions.question: 1 (a) The electronic structures of five atoms, A, B, C, D and E, are shown. ABCDE Answer the following questions about these atoms. Each atom may be used once, more than once or not at all. Which atom, A, B, C, D or E, (i) has a total of 8 electrons, .............................. [1] (ii) is in Group III of the Periodic Table, .............................. [1] (iii) has
questions.text: 1 (a) The electronic structures of five atoms, A, B, C, D and E, are shown. ABCDE Answer the following questions about these atoms. Each atom may be used once, more than once or not at all. Which atom, A, B, C, D or E, (i) has a total of 8 electrons, .............................. [1] (ii) is in Group III of the Periodic Table, .............................. [1] (iii) has

parts.answer: number of electrons in O – ion = 10 number of neutrons in S = 18 number of protons in S = 16 AND in O – ion = 8 0620/32 May/June 2017 / Na + lilac colour

questions.question: 2 (a) The table shows the ions present in a 1000 cm 3 sample of rainwater. ion presentformula of ion mass present in mg / 1000 cm 3 calciumCa 2+ 1 hydrogencarbonateHCO 3 – 3 magnesiumMg 2+ 1 potassiumK + 2 sodiumNa + 9 silicateSiO 3 2– 1 Cl – 17 Answer these questions using the information from the table. (i) State the name of the ion Cl – . .......................................

parts.answer: negative electrode: potassium / K positive electrode: bromine / Br 0620/32 May/June 2017

any 5 of: X has ionic bonding / ionic X particles are regularly arranged / lattice / in rows / uniformly arranged X particles (only) vibrate / do not move from place to place Y has covalent bonding Y has irregular arrangement of particles / random arrangement Y particles are sliding over each other / moving slowly Z has covalent bonding Z particles are randomly arranged / irregularly arranged Z particles moving randomly / moving rapidly / moving freely / moving quickly / moving fast / volume gets larger particles get further apart to blue

it has (two different types of) atoms bonded / joined 0620/32 May/June 2017 it is (very) reactive / too reactive / above carbon in the reactivity series / more reactive than carbon gas syringe connected to a flask OR this described in words closed apparatus / workable apparatus OR this described in words timer or stopwatch OR this described in words / car bodies low density

any 2 advantages: • saves energy • saves mining of ore • saves other finite resources • saves transport costs of bringing ore to factory • reduces pollution(due to dust or exhaust fumes etc.) reactant level below product level / reactants have less energy than products / products have more energy than reactants 0620/32 May/June 2017 circle around carboxylic acid group H O

alcohol group shown as O–H rest of the structure correct how easily it evaporates / boils any value between 65 and 98 (°C) (exclusive of these values) / vapour °C is above the boiling point O) O IF full credit is not awarded, allow 1 mark for (C =) 12, (O =) 16 and (H =) 1 0620/32 May/June 2017

any 3 from: • high melting point / high boiling point • high density • forms coloured compounds / compounds are coloured / ions are coloured • has more than one oxidation state / forms ions with different charges • forms complex ions • catalyst • hard / strong • sonorous / rings (when hit)

gold < copper < iron < potassium IF full credit is not awarded, allow 1 mark for either a correct sequence apart from a consecutive pair reversed OR for the whole sequence reversed the higher the concentration the faster the rate / the lower the concentration the slower the rate / as the concentration increases the rate of reaction increases any value between 45 and 102 hours (exclusive of these values) 0620/32 May/June 2017
(substance containing) only one type of atom / volcanoes / crude oil / petroleum suitable use, e.g. (making) sulfuric acid / making SO / dusting plants / vulcanising rubber / subliming / sublime

any 2 sources: • sulfur dioxide: from volcanoes / burning fossil fuels • oxides of nitrogen: from car exhausts / high temperature furnaces / lightning any 3 effects: • sulfur dioxide: acid rain / named effects of acid rain • sulfur dioxide: irritates eyes or skin • oxides of nitrogen: acid rain / named effect of acid rain • oxides of nitrogen: breathing difficulties / breathing problems / irritates eyes / skin / photochemical smog
add hydrochloric acid to the mixture filter off the sulfur / sulfur on filter paper wash sulfur (with water or other solvent) AND dry in an oven / air dry / leave to dry (in air)
